extra.message: Server-Side Template Injection via Jinja2. User-controlled 'name' query parameter (line 19) is interpolated into an f-string (line 38) to build HTML content, which is then passed to Template(content).render() (line 41). An attacker can inject Jinja2 template syntax (e.g., ?name={{7*6}}) to execute arbitrary expressions and achieve Remote Code Execution.

cwe: CWE-1336: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in a Templating Engine
CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code

extra.message: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF). The PUBLIC_IP_SERVICE_URL is loaded from an environment variable (config.py line 11) with no URL validation, allowlist, or sanitization. requests.get(config.PUBLIC_IP_SERVICE_URL) is called directly on line 31. If an attacker can control this environment variable (via container orchestration misconfiguration, .env manipulation, or other means), they can force the server to make requests to arbitrary internal or external URLs, potentially accessing sensitive services.

Hardcoded sensitive credentials found. SUPER_SECRET_NAME and SUPER_SECRET_TOKEN are hardcoded in the source code (lines 13-15) instead of being loaded from environment variables. These secrets are visible in version control, logs, and source code repositories. The FIXME comments suggest the developer was aware this should be changed to use os.getenv().
